rationale: In the citing paper, Hoban and Bucklin (2015) is discussed with specific claims, not only listed in a generic citation cluster. The authors use it to frame that ad effectiveness can vary by purchase-funnel stage and then explicitly compare their own heterogeneity findings to that prior pattern. They also reference it in discussing how prior firm engagement may moderate responses to online promotions. This gives the citation a meaningful interpretive and positioning role.
relation_description: The citing study uses Hoban and Bucklin (2015) as a prior empirical benchmark for stage-dependent online ad effects and engagement-based heterogeneity. It leverages those findings to contextualize and interpret its own Facebook-like and boosted-post results rather than merely mentioning the paper in passing.
